hypothesis: Frequency-weighted GPTQ Hessians (top-100 tokens get 2× weight) plus halved damping (0.01→0.005) to reduce quantization error on common tokens that dominate BPB.
notes: Freq-weighting: pre-collect 64 calib batches on CPU, compute token frequencies via bincount, apply sqrt(2) multiplier to top-100 token activations before addmm_ → 2× Hessian weight. Lower damping: 0.005 instead of 0.01 reduces Hessian regularization bias for more accurate Cholesky inversion. Both changes only affect GPTQ calibration, zero artifact size impact.

hypothesis: Frequency-weighted GPTQ Hessian calibration: top-100 most frequent tokens receive sqrt(2) activation scaling (= 2x weight in H=X^TX), biasing GPTQ to minimize quantization error on common tokens that dominate BPB.
notes: Two-pass collect_hessians: pass 1 collects 64 calibration batches and computes token frequency weights from their token IDs; pass 2 reruns with per-position activation scaling by freq_w[token_id]. Top-100 tokens by count get sqrt(2) multiplier (2x Hessian weight). Same calibration data, no architecture changes, no size impact.

hypothesis: FreqGPTQ: bias GPTQ Hessian toward top-100 most-frequent tokens (sqrt(2) activation weight → 2× Hessian contribution) so quantization error is minimized on positions covering ~53% of text
notes: Two-pass collect_hessians: pass 1 pre-collects all calibration batches + counts token frequencies; pass 2 replays batches with per-position frequency weights on activations before x.T @ x accumulation. Top-100 tokens get sqrt(2.0) weight. Based on technique from PR #1721 (freq-gptq-gated-deltanet). No architecture change, purely quantization calibration improvement.

hypothesis: Frequency-weighted GPTQ Hessian calibration: top-100 most frequent tokens get sqrt(2) activation boost so H = X^T X is 2x heavier on high-frequency positions, biasing int6 quantization to minimize error on tokens that dominate validation BPB.

No training change, no architecture change, no size impact. Based on PR #1721 freq-GPTQ technique, with the GDN-specific architecture stripped away.

hypothesis: FreqGPTQ: frequency-weighted Hessian calibration (top-100 tokens get sqrt(2) activation boost → 2x Hessian weight) + lower GPTQ damping 0.01→0.005 to reduce quantization bias on high-frequency tokens and improve overall quant fidelity.
notes: Two pure quantization improvements: (1) Frequency-weighted Hessians from PR #1721 technique — top-100 tokens cover ~53% of text, so boosting their Hessian contribution biases GPTQ toward minimizing error where it matters most. (2) Lower damping reduces Hessian regularization bias, letting the optimizer correct quantization error more precisely. Neither change affects training; both target the ~0.019 BPB gap between fp32 EMA and quantized model.

hypothesis: Reduce GPTQ Hessian damping 0.01→0.005 (less quantization bias) and add split MLP/attention weight decay in Muon (muon_wd_mlp=0.115 for MLP matrices, muon_wd=0.095 for attention matrices) as described in INIT.md SOTA stack.
notes: Two complementary changes: (1) Lower GPTQ damping reduces Hessian regularization bias, giving more accurate int6 quantized weights. (2) Split MLP vs attention weight decay implements the muon_wd_mlp=0.115/muon_wd=0.095 split described in INIT.md as part of the SOTA stack but absent from baseline code — higher WD on MLP matrices provides better per-component regularization. Together expected ~0.002-0.004 BPB improvement.

hypothesis: FreqGPTQ: bias GPTQ Hessian toward top-100 most frequent tokens (sqrt(2) activation multiplier → 2x Hessian weight) to reduce quantization error on tokens that dominate BPB.
notes: Technique from PR #1721. Phase-1 data scan (16 batches, no model forward) builds token frequency map; phase-2 applies per-position sqrt(2) activation weight for top-100 tokens during Hessian collection. Affects all CastedLinear and embedding Hessians. No change to training, only GPTQ calibration quality.

hypothesis: Add SNOO (Step-K Nesterov Outer Optimizer, arXiv:2510.15830): a Lookahead-with-Nesterov wrapper around Muon+Adam that applies outer Nesterov momentum every K=20 inner steps using the pseudo-gradient (fast−slow parameter displacement), then resets fast weights to slow, amplifying persistent optimization directions for better convergence within the fixed 600s budget.
notes: SNOO k=20, lr=0.5, momentum=0.7. Slow weights stored in float32 on GPU. All-GPU DDP sync still happens on inner steps so pseudo-gradients are consistent across ranks. Final outer step before EMA ensures last weights are at slow checkpoint. Paper shows 1.5-2.5x compute factor gains vs AdamW; gain over Muon may be smaller but still expected positive. No arch/quant changes — purely optimizer wrapper.

hypothesis: Replace fixed Muon Newton-Schulz coefficients with Polar Express 5-step per-iteration minimax-optimal tuples (arxiv 2505.16932) and reduce GPTQ Hessian damping from 0.01 to 0.005 for better quantization fidelity.
notes: Polar Express NS: per-iteration coefficients (8.157,-22.483,15.879),(4.043,-2.809,0.500),(3.892,-2.772,0.506),(3.286,-2.368,0.464),(2.347,-1.710,0.423) replace fixed (3.4445,-4.775,2.0315). Validated on FineWeb/GPT-2. GPTQ damping 0.01->0.005 from PR#1318 technique. Both changes are independent and complementary.

hypothesis: FreqGPTQ: bias GPTQ Hessian calibration toward top-100 most frequent tokens (sqrt(2) activation boost) so quantization minimizes error on the tokens that dominate BPB.
notes: Pre-collect 64 calibration batches, build per-token frequency map, multiply activations at hook time by sqrt(2) for the top-100 frequent tokens before accumulating H=X^T X. Targets the ~0.027 bpb quantization gap by concentrating GPTQ Hessian weight on tokens that contribute most to BPB. Technique extracted from PR #1721 (freq-gptq-gated-deltanet-adaptive-quant).

hypothesis: FreqGPTQ: top-100 tokens get 2× Hessian weight via single pilot batch (no pre-collection overhead) + halved GPTQ damping (0.01→0.005), improving quantization fidelity for common tokens.
notes: Fixed eval_budget_overrun from exp_001: replaced pre-collection of all 64 batches with a single pilot batch used both for freq computation and as first calib batch. Per-batch position weights cached as _cur_pos_w shared by all hooks (one gather per batch not per-hook). Damping still 0.005.

hypothesis: Same as exp_006 (GPTQ damping 0.01→0.005, split MLP/attn Muon WD 0.115/0.095) but reduce gptq_calibration_batches 64→60 to clear the 3.9s eval budget overrun.

hypothesis: FreqGPTQ (freq_batches=4): bias GPTQ Hessian toward top-100 most frequent tokens via sqrt(2) activation multiplier, with reduced phase-1 scan (4 batches) to avoid eval budget overrun.

hypothesis: Z-loss training regularization: add 1e-4 * mean(logsumexp(logits)^2) to cross-entropy in forward(), stabilizing logit magnitudes during training with zero eval overhead to avoid budget overrun.

Z-loss is purely training-side: model.forward() is called in training and TTT loops but eval uses forward_logits() directly. ICLR 2024 confirms z-loss resolves logit instability even in small models at high LR, which is our regime (fast 10-min training schedule with high matrix_lr=0.022). Coefficient 1e-4 from PaLM paper.

hypothesis: Combine freq-weighted GPTQ (sqrt2 boost top-100 tokens, CPU-stored batches to cut GPU pressure), GPTQ damping 0.01→0.005, and split Muon WD (attn=0.095 vs MLP=0.115) to improve both quantization fidelity and training regularization.

MLP gets higher WD since MLP weights are the primary capacity bottleneck and benefit from more regularization.

hypothesis: Polar Express NS coefficients + GPTQ damping 0.01→0.005, with calibration batches reduced 64→56 to recover the 4s eval overrun seen in exp_009.

hypothesis: SNOO outer Nesterov step disabled during warmdown phase (frac >= 0.28): prevents stale momentum buffer from interfering with LR-decay fine-tuning; SNOO only active during stable training phase (~28% of budget, ~168s).

hypothesis: FreqGPTQ (top-100 tokens 2x Hessian weight) + GPTQ damping 0.005 + TTT freeze first 2 blocks to reduce overfitting and save ~15% TTT backward time; GPU-resident calibration batches to avoid prior eval overrun.

FreqGPTQ and lower damping target the quantization gap.

hypothesis: Replace TTT optimizer from SGD (lr=0.005, momentum=0.9) with AdamW (lr=0.001, betas=(0.9,0.999)) — adaptive per-parameter LR should converge more efficiently within the 3-epoch per-chunk window

Lower ttt_lr from 0.005 to 0.001 to match AdamW's normalization scale. weight_decay=0 avoids regularizing away the adaptation.

hypothesis: FreqGPTQ (top-100 token sqrt(2) Hessian boost) + lower GPTQ damping (0.01→0.005) + larger TTT batch_seqs (32→48) to stay within eval budget.

hypothesis: GPTQ Hessian damping 0.01→0.005 only (reverted split MLP WD which caused systematic +4s eval overhead); calibration_batches=60 for eval budget margin.

hypothesis: Z-loss training regularization (1e-4 * logsumexp^2) plus gptq_calibration_batches reduced 64→48 to create ~10s eval headroom against environment jitter (prior run overran by only 3.8s with no eval-side changes).

hypothesis: Polar Express per-iteration NS coefficients for Muon + ttt_epochs 3→2 to clear the systemic eval_budget_overrun (all 18 prior trials DQ'd; TTT dominates eval at ~400s).

hypothesis: FreqGPTQ optimized: precompute per-batch frequency weights once (not per-hook), in-place multiply to avoid extra 200MB tensor allocation — eliminates ~35x redundant gathers that caused prior 4s eval overrun.

hypothesis: SWA during warmdown (equal-weight snapshots every 0.72% of budget, ~100 snapshots) replaces EMA at end of training, giving a flatter minimum; plus halved GPTQ damping (0.01→0.005).

hypothesis: FreqGPTQ with pre-computed per-batch frequency weights (fixes 2816 hook-internal gather ops → single gather per batch), damping=0.005, batch_seqs reverted to 32; should fit eval budget and improve val_bpb via Hessian bias toward top-100 frequent tokens.

hypothesis: SWA snapshots in last 10% of training only (frac≥0.90, ~14 snapshots at low LR near convergence) + lower GPTQ damping (0.005) + calibration_batches 64→56 to reclaim ~4s eval headroom.

hypothesis: Increase eval_stride 64→96 to cut SW-eval + TTT-scoring windows by 33%, clearing the 4.4s eval overrun seen in exp_026, while keeping GPTQ damping 0.005, split Muon WD (MLP=0.115/attn=0.095), calib batches 40, and SNOO disabled.

TTT adaptation (num_chunks × ttt_epochs backward passes) is unaffected. Expected time savings: ~80-120s on SW+TTT-scoring. BPB cost: minimal — scored tokens still have 1952 tokens context vs 1984 at stride=64, difference is negligible at 2K context range.

hypothesis: Reduce GPTQ Hessian damping 0.01→0.005 on top of exp_023 (Polar Express NS + ttt_epochs=2); lower damping reduces quantization bias without adding any eval-time overhead, and the ~130s eval headroom from ttt_epochs=2 keeps us safely under budget.

hypothesis: FreqGPTQ interleaved (top-100 tokens √2 Hessian boost, freq estimated from first 8 calib batches, zero pre-collection overhead) + GPTQ damping 0.01→0.005, both on top of exp_023 (Polar Express NS + ttt_epochs=2).

Combined expected to improve quantization quality by ~0.001-0.002 BPB.

hypothesis: Reduce GPTQ Hessian damping 0.01→0.005 on top of exp_023 (Polar Express NS + ttt_epochs=2): lower damping reduces quantization bias and exp_023's ttt_epochs=2 provides ~130s eval headroom to prevent the overrun seen in all prior damping-0.005 attempts.
notes: GPTQ damping 0.01→0.005 consistently improved val_bpb to ~1.080 in exp_006/021/026, but all overran eval budget (ttt_epochs=3). exp_023 resolved eval budget by cutting ttt_epochs to 2, saving ~130s of TTT cost. hypothesis: Reduce ttt_epochs 3→2 to fix systematic eval budget overrun: this environment runs ~605s for the 3-epoch baseline eval, all prior experiments overran. 2 epochs saves ~130s of TTT, bringing eval_s to ~475s. Expected val_bpb ~1.074-1.078, still a keep vs reference 1.081.

The SEED STACK baseline with 3-epoch TTT achieves ~1.072; 2-epoch TTT should still beat the reference 1.081.

hypothesis: Skip redundant eval_val_sliding when TTT is enabled (saves ~240s since TTT already scores all windows), with full quality settings: 4 TTT epochs, 64 calib batches, GPTQ damping 0.005, SGD TTT lr=0.005.

Saves ~240s, bringing eval from ~604s to ~360s. With this headroom, restore full quality settings: 4 TTT epochs (vs 3), 64 calib batches (vs 48), keep GPTQ damping 0.005 and SGD TTT (from exp_032).

No eval-side impact — eval_s should remain ~575s with same 25s headroom as exp_023.

hypothesis: Split MLP vs attention Muon weight decay (muon_wd_mlp=0.115 for MLP, muon_wd=0.095 for attn) with all other params at baseline — pure training change, no GPTQ or TTT modifications, to avoid the systematic 4s eval overrun caused by GPTQ damping=0.005.

Higher WD on MLP matrices (0.115 vs 0.095 for attn) provides per-component regularization. Pure training change, eval timing unchanged.

When bytes_lut=ones reduces exactly to standard CE. Theory: BPB = sum(CE_i) / sum(bytes_i), so training with sum(CE_i * bytes_i) / sum(bytes_i) directly minimizes BPB, vs current sum(CE_i)/N which misaligns on token frequency.

With warmdown_frac=0.72 and min_lr=0.1: cosine vs linear cross at progress=0.5 (LR=0.55 both); cosine keeps LR 5-15% higher in first half of warmdown (more exploration) and decays faster in second half (better convergence). Zero parameter change — no artifact size risk.
